description: GatewayZ Distributed Tracing - Span metrics (RED), provider/model performance from traces, and trace investigation via OpenTelemetry and Tempo

options.content:
## GatewayZ — Distributed Tracing

Span metrics (RED), AI provider/model performance from traces, and live TraceQL analytics. Powered by Tempo 2.6.1 with local_blocks processor.

| Signal | Datasource | What It Shows |
|--------|-----------|---------------|
| 📊 **Span Calls Rate** | Mimir | requests/s from trace spans — complements Prometheus metrics |
| ❌ **Span Error Rate** | Mimir | error rate by provider/model from OTel status codes |
| ⏱️ **P95 Span Latency** | Mimir | end-to-end latency histograms (buckets to 120s for large models) |
| 🔴 **Live Span Rate** | Tempo (TraceQL) | real-time rate() from local_blocks — no Mimir lag |
| 🗂️ **Token Usage** | Tempo (TraceQL) | prompt + completion tokens from span attributes |
| 🗺️ **Service Topology** | Tempo (serviceMap) | gatewayz-backend → api.openai.com edges via peer_attributes |

⚠️ **BACKEND-2 required:** All panels show no data until gatewayz-backend sends OTLP traces to `tempo.railway.internal:4317`. See the [Four Golden Signals](#) service graph explanation.

_Navigate: [Four Golden Signals](#) for golden signals SLO · [AI Provider Intelligence](#) for provider health · [AI Model Analytics](#) for token usage_

panels.description: Rate of span calls over time grouped by operation name. Shows which operations are most frequently called.
panels.title: Request Rate by Operation (Top 10)

panels.description: Error rate over time by operation. Highlights which operations are producing the most errors.
panels.title: Error Rate by Operation (Top 10)

panels.description: P95 latency over time by operation. Shows which operations are the slowest.
panels.title: P95 Latency by Operation (Top 10)
AI Provider & Model Performance (from Span Metrics)

panels.description: Request rate per AI provider derived from span metrics. Shows which providers handle the most traffic.
panels.title: Request Rate by AI Provider

panels.description: P95 latency by AI provider from span metrics. Highlights which providers are slowest.
panels.title: P95 Latency by AI Provider

panels.description: Request rate per AI model from span metrics. Shows which models are most frequently used via traces.
panels.title: Request Rate by Model (Top 15)

panels.description: P95 latency per AI model from span metrics. Identifies slow models (e.g. DeepSeek, large context models).
panels.title: P95 Latency by Model (Top 15)

panels.description: Error rate by provider from span metrics. Quickly identifies which providers are having issues.
panels.title: Error Rate by AI Provider

panels.description: Visual service dependency graph generated by Tempo's built-in service-graph processor. Each node represents a service; edges show call volume and error rate. Red arc = failing calls; green arc = successful calls. Click a node to drill into traces for that service.
panels.title: Live Service Dependency Graph

panels.description: Heatmap of all span durations (histogram buckets from Tempo span-metrics). Bright bands = high request density at that latency. Sudden shifts upward indicate latency regressions. Each row = one histogram bucket boundary (le). Filter by service or operation using the variables above.
panels.title: Span Duration Heatmap — Latency Distribution Over Time

panels.description: Every point is a traced request. X-axis = time; Y-axis = duration. Click any point to open the full distributed trace waterfall/flame graph. Red points = error traces. Large Y values = slow traces worth investigating.
panels.title: Error Trace Scatter — Duration vs Time (click to open waterfall)
🔥  CPU Profiling Correlation — Flamegraph + Provider Breakdown
📜  Log Context — Errors Time-Synced to Trace Window
TTFC Span P95 by AI Provider (from Mimir)
AI Parameter Analysis -- Most Requested Models & Operations
Operation Type Breakdown -- AI Operations by Rate
Error Hotspot -- Operations by Failure Rate
Error Rate Trend -- Top 5 Failing Operations
Multi-Operation Error Overlay -- Concurrent Failures
panels.description: When multiple series spike at the same time, errors are happening in parallel across operations -- a systemic issue, not an isolated one.

panels.title: Recent Error Traces -- Concurrent View (sort by start time to spot clustering)
panels.description: Sort this table by Start Time to see whether errors cluster together in time. Simultaneous errors across different rootName values indicate a systemic failure rather than an isolated bug.

panels.title: 🩺  Tracing Pipeline Health — Span Ingestion · Metrics · Error Rate · Service Breakdown
Trace Ingestion Rate
panels.description: Spans/s arriving at Tempo's distributor. Goes to zero = backend stopped emitting traces.

panels.title: Span Metrics Rate
panels.description: Rate of span metrics being computed from traces. These power the RED metrics in this dashboard.

panels.title: Span Duration P95 — All Operations
panels.description: P95 latency across all span types. Red = system-wide slowdown, not just one operation.

panels.title: Span Error Rate — Proportion of Error Spans
panels.description: Fraction of all spans with ERROR status code. >5% = systemic issue. Complements the four golden signals error rate.

panels.title: Trace Calls by Service — Which Service Generates Most Spans
panels.description: Call rate per service from span metrics. A drop in gatewayz-backend = traces stopped flowing from the main app.

panels.title: Span Latency by Service (P95) — Cross-Service Latency Comparison
panels.description: P95 span duration per service. Compare services side-by-side to identify which is the latency bottleneck.

panels.title: Advanced Analytics — TraceQL Metrics & Token Intelligence
panels.description: Live analytics computed directly from Tempo trace blocks using TraceQL aggregate syntax. Panels 701–703 require the local_blocks processor. Panels 704–705 require Phase 2 span metric dimensions.

panels.title: Live Span Rate by Provider (TraceQL Metrics)
panels.description: Real-time span ingestion rate per AI provider computed directly from Tempo local blocks using TraceQL aggregate syntax. Unlike Mimir-backed panels this reads from local Parquet blocks (accurate to ~30s). Requires local_blocks processor.

panels.title: Live Error Span Rate by Model (TraceQL Metrics)
panels.description: Error span rate computed directly from Tempo local blocks. Filters to spans with status=error and groups by AI model. Shows which models are currently failing. Requires local_blocks processor.

panels.title: Token Usage Rate by Model (from Traces)
panels.description: Prompt and completion token consumption rate per model. Uses TraceQL to read gen_ai.usage.prompt_tokens and gen_ai.usage.completion_tokens directly from Tempo trace spans — NOT from Mimir labels (token counts are numeric, not categorical). Requires local_blocks processor. Values are approximate based on trace sample window.

panels.title: Completion Finish Reason Distribution by Model
panels.description: Breakdown of how LLM completions terminate: stop=normal, length=truncated (context/token limit hit), tool_calls=function call triggered, content_filter=blocked by moderation. High "length" percentage means callers send over-long prompts or max_tokens is too low. Requires gen_ai.response.finish_reason dimension (Phase 2).

panels.title: Error Rate by HTTP Route (Span Metrics)
panels.description: Per-endpoint span error rate from Mimir span metrics. Isolates which API route is responsible for errors — high error rate on /v1/stream but not /v1/chat/completions indicates a streaming-specific bug. Requires http.route dimension (Phase 2).

panels.title: Service Dependency Topology (from Service Graphs)
panels.description: Auto-generated service map from Tempo service_graphs processor. Nodes represent services and external dependencies. Edge weight = request rate; red edges = error-heavy paths. External provider nodes (api.openai.com, api.anthropic.com etc.) appear after peer_attributes are active (Phase 1 service_graphs enhancement). Datasource: grafana_tempo → serviceMap → reads traces_service_graph_* from Mimir.

title: GatewayZ Distributed Tracing
